auditor: Claude Fable 5 + Opus remediation subagent, two-lens audit (runtime/cli + docs/ci/release)
scope: Final audit-and-improvement pass over ai-agent-notifier: two verified runtime bugs, honesty of the user-facing README, real install bootstrappers, doctor --deep candor, CI double-run economics, small CLI/runtime correctness fixes, and the missing npm release pipeline. Reinstates the docs/audits/ convention that lapsed while demand passes 3-5 (PRs #5-#8) shipped without records.

checks_passed: npm test — full unit + integration suite green locally on Windows (308 tests, 300 pass, 8 platform-skipped, 0 fail), including all new regression tests
linux buildNotifySendArgs: options precede the `--` guard; a leading-dash message lands after `--` (tests/platforms.test.mjs)
sendNtfy resolves false (never throws) on a scheme-less server typo (tests/ntfy-send.test.mjs)
config-loader rejects a control-char (newline) source label and non-string label/icon, keeps clean custom values (tests/config-loader-validation.test.mjs)
cli test <unknown-channel> exits 1 naming the valid set (tests/cli-test.test.mjs)
doctor --deep on a non-darwin platform emits an explicit 'not available' info line (tests/doctor.test.mjs)
isNewer semver gate: true only when strictly newer; 1.0.6 vs local 1.2.1 stays quiet (tests/update-check.test.mjs)
npm version 1.2.1 --no-git-tag-version synced package.json + plugin.json + marketplace.json to 1.2.1 with no tag created
blocked: npm publish of v1.2.1 — requires the maintainer to add the NPM_TOKEN repo secret and push a v1.2.1 tag; CI must never publish or tag itself (release.yml is the rail, deliberately un-triggered)
Real WSL toast-to-Windows-host delivery — not provable on a hosted GitHub runner (cannot host a WSL guest and an interactive Windows desktop together); documented as a proof boundary instead
Linux doctor --deep daemon read-back — CLOSED in the follow-up post-audit hardening pass (TC-27 now 'fixed': dunst-history read-back probe + CI gate on toast-linux.yml)

findings.title: Linux notify-send called with positionals before the `--` end-of-options guard
findings.evidence: src/platforms/linux.mjs:27-31 — args were [title, message, '--urgency', level] with no `--`. GLib GOption parses any pre-`--` arg starting with '-' as an unknown option, so a rich-content message like '- Fixed the bug' makes notify-send exit non-zero and the toast silently never fires.
findings.why_it_matters: Claude Code rich content puts the assistant's own text into the body; a dash-leading line is plausible and would silently drop the notification.
findings.resolution: Extracted exported buildNotifySendArgs(notification, iconPath): ['--urgency', level, ...(icon?['--icon',icon]:[]), '--', title, message] — all options first, then `--`, then positionals. URGENCY_MAP/priority behavior unchanged.
findings.verification: tests/platforms.test.mjs asserts arg order and that a leading-dash message lands after `--`.

findings.title: sendNtfy `new URL()` unguarded — violates the module's resolve-false/never-throw contract
findings.evidence: src/ntfy.mjs:31 — `const parsed = new URL(url)` was unguarded, unlike webhook.mjs:63-70 and sentry.mjs:75. A server typo with no scheme ('ntfy.sh') throws 'Invalid URL', crashing `aan test ntfy` with a raw stack instead of a clean false.
findings.why_it_matters: The channel modules promise never to throw so a bad config can't take down the hook or the CLI; ntfy was the one that could.
findings.resolution: Wrapped `new URL(url)` in try/catch → logHookError + resolve(false) naming the bad server (not the topic). Also wrapped transport.request setup in try/catch to mirror webhook.mjs's guard against synchronous throws on non-http(s) protocols/invalid headers.
findings.verification: tests/ntfy-send.test.mjs: a scheme-less server yields false with no throw.

findings.title: README standalone install one-liners point at nonexistent files
findings.evidence: README.md:134 (setup/install.ps1) and :139 (setup/install.sh) were advertised as curl|iex bootstrappers, but setup/ contained only patch-config.mjs — both URLs 404'd.
findings.why_it_matters: A trust-sensitive install path people are told to pipe into a shell must exist and work; a 404 (or worse, a silent no-op) is a broken first impression.
findings.resolution: Created setup/install.sh (set -euo pipefail, fails loudly) and setup/install.ps1 ($ErrorActionPreference='Stop'): both verify Node>=18 + npm with an actionable per-OS hint, then exec `npx --yes ai-agent-notifier@latest setup` forwarding args. setup/ is already in package.json files[].
findings.verification: Files created and readable; scripts are thin/no-network until the npx handoff. Piped-shell end-to-end run is a maintainer step after publish.

findings.title: README claims a macOS terminal-notifier fallback that does not exist in code
findings.evidence: README.md:307 — 'Falls back to terminal-notifier for richer features if available'. grep for terminal-notifier across src/ and cli/ returns nothing; macOS uses osascript only.
findings.why_it_matters: The repo's extreme-honesty culture requires README claims to match code exactly.
findings.resolution: Deleted the claim (did not implement terminal-notifier).

findings.title: WSL over-claim: real cross-boundary toast delivery is asserted but never proven
findings.evidence: README.md:51 ('real Windows toast notifications from inside WSL') and :314-319. Only detection + interop invocation are tested (tests/platforms-wsl.test.mjs, fully deps-injected); no CI proves a toast reaches the Windows host.
findings.why_it_matters: Matches how the repo already scopes Codex exec vs the TUI approval loop — claims must stop at what CI proves.
findings.resolution: Softened the feature bullet and Platform Details WSL section to 'routed to Windows over PowerShell interop' and added an explicit proof-boundary note in both the WSL section and the Testing footnotes explaining WSL has no table row on purpose.

findings.title: doctor --deep silently no-ops on linux/win32
findings.evidence: cli/doctor-checks.mjs — the `deep` flag is only consumed by the darwin toastAuthCheck; on other platforms it was accepted and ignored with no output.
findings.why_it_matters: A flag that silently does nothing misleads the user into thinking a deep verification ran.
findings.resolution: runChecks now pushes an explicit info-status row 'deep verification not available on <platform> (static checks only)' when --deep is passed on a platform with no deep probe; doctor.mjs ICON map gained an 'info' glyph; --json stays machine-parseable (new id 'deep-probe').
findings.verification: tests/doctor.test.mjs asserts the deep-probe info row on non-darwin.

findings.title: `aan test <unknown-channel>` prints the header and exits 0 doing nothing
findings.evidence: cli/test.mjs — an unrecognised channel matched none of the do* gates, so the command printed the header and exited 0, reading as a silent success.
findings.why_it_matters: A typo'd channel should fail loudly, not look like it worked.
findings.resolution: Added exported KNOWN_CHANNELS = [toast, ntfy, webhook, bell, both]; an unknown channel prints 'Unknown channel: X (valid: ...)' to stderr and exits 1. NOTE: the task-suggested valid set listed 'all', but the code + README:147 use 'both' (no-arg = every channel); recorded the honest set that matches the code.
findings.verification: tests/cli-test.test.mjs asserts exit 1 + message; KNOWN_CHANNELS matches the documented set.

findings.title: Hook stdout.write followed by immediate process.exit can drop the response
findings.evidence: src/notify.mjs:201-202 — process.stdout.write(responseBody); process.exit(0). On a back-pressured stdout the buffered write can be lost before exit (notably the claude terminalSequence bell payload).
findings.resolution: process.stdout.write(responseBody, () => process.exit(0)) — exit only after the write drains.

findings.title: config `sources` block had no schema check — label/icon unvalidated
findings.evidence: src/config-loader.mjs — 'sources' was in knownTop but had no checkBlock; a newline in sources.<tool>.label flows to notification.title and later corrupts the ntfy Title HTTP header at send time.
findings.why_it_matters: A control char in label/icon is a header-injection / send-failure vector; every other block is validated.
findings.resolution: Added a nested sources validator (like events): each sources.<tool> must be an object; label/icon must be strings with no control chars (charCodeAt < 0x20 or === 0x7f), wrong types deleted+reported so defaults win; unknown per-tool keys reported but kept.
findings.verification: tests/config-loader-validation.test.mjs: non-string label reverted, newline label rejected, unknown key kept, clean custom label passes.

findings.title: macOS NC DB path interpolated into a sqlite file: URI without percent-encoding
findings.evidence: src/platforms/macos-delivery.mjs:103,115 — `file:${dbPath}?mode=ro` / `file:${copy}?mode=ro`; a space in the home dir (/Users/Jane Doe/...) corrupts the URI.
findings.resolution: Wrapped both interpolations in encodeURI() (keeps the `/` separators, encodes spaces).

findings.title: update-check compares versions with string `!==`, not a semver newer-than
findings.evidence: cli/update-check.mjs:63 — `latest !== pkg.version`. With npm latest at 1.0.6 and the repo at 1.2.1, this reports 1.0.6 as an available 'update', nagging users to downgrade.
findings.why_it_matters: Directly wrong right now given the npm/repo version gap this pass also addresses.
findings.resolution: Added exported isNewer(a,b): field-by-field numeric semver compare (1.10.0 > 1.9.0), pre-release/build ignored, unparseable treated as not-newer; fetchLatest now resolves only when strictly newer. ~11 lines.
findings.verification: tests/update-check.test.mjs: strictly-newer true, equal/older false (1.0.6 vs 1.2.1), garbage false.

findings.title: CI lanes run twice on same-repo PR branches (bare push + pull_request)
findings.evidence: unit.yml, e2e.yml, agents.yml, toast-linux.yml, toast-macos.yml, toast-native.yml triggered on bare `push:` + `pull_request:`; concurrency keyed on github.ref does not dedupe push vs PR refs, so a same-repo PR branch ran every lane twice. agents.yml also ran the full 3-OS install matrix on docs-only commits.
findings.resolution: Restricted `push:` to `branches: [main]` on all six (mirroring the live-* lanes); pull_request unchanged. Added the shared paths-ignore ['**/*.md','docs/**','LICENSE','.gitignore'] to agents.yml on both push and pull_request.
findings.verification: YAML reviewed against the live-*.yml pattern already in the repo.

findings.title: No npm release pipeline; npm latest (1.0.6) far behind the repo (1.2.0); zero tags/releases
findings.evidence: package.json version was 1.2.0 vs npm 1.0.6; no .github/workflows/release.yml; no git tags or GitHub releases.
findings.why_it_matters: Users installing from npm get a stale build; there was no automated, provenance-signed path to publish.
findings.resolution: Added .github/workflows/release.yml (triggers on push of tag v*): (a) 3-OS x node 18/20/22 unit matrix — npm test also covers the unit-safe toast tests (live paths gated behind AAN_TOAST_LIVE, unset); (b) tag/version match + plugin-manifest lockstep + `npm pack --dry-run`; (c) `npm publish --provenance --access public` with id-token: write + NPM_TOKEN; (d) `gh release create --verify-tag --generate-notes` with contents: write. Bumped to 1.2.1 via `npm version 1.2.1 --no-git-tag-version` (ran the repo's sync-plugin-version.mjs, syncing plugin.json + marketplace.json). No tag created, nothing published — the maintainer cuts the tag after adding NPM_TOKEN.
findings.verification: Version sync confirmed (package.json/plugin.json/marketplace.json all 1.2.1); release.yml is deliberately un-triggered until a v* tag is pushed.

findings.title: docs/audits/ convention lapsed — demand passes 3-5 shipped with no audit record
findings.evidence: docs/audits/ held only 2026-07-09-second-pass.json + schema.json; PRs #5-#8 (1.1.0 five-feature pass, macOS/Windows/Linux proof passes) landed without records.
findings.resolution: Wrote this record (docs/audits/2026-07-16-final-pass.json) against the existing schema, capturing all findings from both audit lenses.

findings.title: windows.mjs has no `--` parity for leading-dash content (parallel to RT-16)
findings.evidence: The Windows toast backend passes title/message as PowerShell -File parameters. Reviewed for the same dash-parsing risk as Linux.
findings.why_it_matters: Considered because RT-16 was a real dash bug on Linux.
findings.resolution: Accepted. A PowerShell [string] parameter consumes the next token positionally regardless of a leading dash; it only misparses if the message value exactly equals another declared parameter name — not the case for notification bodies. No `--` equivalent needed. Left unchanged.

findings.title: patch-config TOML edits via string surgery (existing TODO CL-18)
findings.evidence: setup/patch-config.mjs performs Codex TOML edits with guarded string manipulation rather than a TOML parser (prior-pass finding CL-18).
findings.resolution: Accepted, re-reviewed. The surgery is guarded and covered by patch-config*.test.mjs; introducing a TOML dependency contradicts the zero-dependency posture. Left as the documented CL-18 TODO.

findings.title: rebuildHooksState / stripHooksStateKeys duplication
findings.evidence: The two helpers share structure but are intentionally separate to keep the corruption-preventing rebuild path isolated.
findings.resolution: Accepted. Unifying them risks reintroducing the exact hooks-state corruption the split prevents; the duplication is deliberate and low-cost. Left unchanged.

findings.title: No CI lane proves real WSL toast delivery to the Windows host
findings.evidence: tests/platforms-wsl.test.mjs covers detection + interop invocation only; end-to-end cross-boundary delivery is unproven.
findings.resolution: Deferred — not feasible on hosted runners (a runner cannot host both a WSL guest and an interactive Windows desktop). Documented as a README proof boundary (MD-18) instead. Revisit only with a self-hosted WSL+desktop runner.

findings.title: npm publish of v1.2.1 to the real user funnel
findings.evidence: npm latest is 1.0.6; release.yml exists but is un-triggered; NPM_TOKEN secret and a v1.2.1 tag are prerequisites.
findings.resolution: Blocked on maintainer: add the NPM_TOKEN repo secret (npm automation token, publish scope), then `git tag v1.2.1 && git push origin v1.2.1` to trigger release.yml. CI must not tag or publish itself.

findings.title: Linux doctor --deep has no daemon read-back probe
findings.evidence: cli/doctor-checks.mjs — --deep only had a real probe on darwin (NC DB read-back). Linux/win32 reported 'not available' (CL-19) rather than silently no-op.
findings.resolution: Closed in the post-audit hardening pass (branch feat/post-audit-hardening). Added linuxDeepToastCheck(): on --deep it fires a real low-urgency test toast through the PRODUCT backend (src/platforms/linux.mjs sendToast, keying urgency off notification.priority) with a nonce, then reads dunst's JSON history (dunstctl close-all + history) and asserts the nonce appears — the Linux analogue of the macOS NC read-back. Where dunstctl is absent (GNOME/KDE), it degrades honestly to an 'info' row ('dispatched-but-unverified'), never a fake pass; where dunstctl is present but the nonce is missing it is a real 'fail'; a notify-send failure is a 'warn'. Deps-injected (hasBin/sendToast/dunstctl/sleepFn) so the four cases are unit-tested off-Linux. --json stays machine-parseable (new id 'toast-deep', which may carry an 'info' status).
findings.verification: tests/doctor.test.mjs: 4 new unit tests (dunstctl-present success, nonce-missing fail, no-dunstctl info, no-notify-send warn). CI leverage: toast-linux.yml now runs the real `aan doctor --deep --json` under xvfb+dbus+dunst and hard-asserts the toast-deep row reports ok — turning the probe into a proven behaviour, not unit-only. Full suite green locally (312 tests, 304 pass, 8 platform-skipped, 0 fail).
